venue: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY

market_positions.selection: No - New York wins by over 13.5 points

quarter_flow.note: Knicks opened efficient, Brunson rhythm established; PHI kept afloat via free throws.
Decisive game break. Knicks won the quarter by 15 and led 74-51 at half.
No regression. Knicks pushed lead to 34 by end of 3Q.
Bench-heavy garbage time; starters sat, Knicks still extended.

model_diagnostics.primary_failure: The model underestimated blowout-extension risk after Knicks shot quality and pace stayed elite through the second quarter.
model_diagnostics.secondary_failure: Paul George rebound over was exposed by role compression and limited minutes due to blowout; he finished with only 3 rebounds in 26:26.
model_diagnostics.successful_read: Knicks offensive superiority and Brunson pressure were correctly identified, but not aggressively enough for margin expansion.

rebound_funnel_review.reason_for_miss: Rebound chances did not funnel to George because Knicks made 63.1% FG and 51.4% from 3, reducing miss volume. Philadelphia also lost starter minutes early due to the 39-point margin.
rebound_funnel_review.future_rule: Do not ladder wing rebounds when opponent projected shot quality exceeds 60% true-shot style pressure or when blowout risk is elevated. Require stable minutes plus miss-volume confirmation.

edge_scoring.notes: Small stakes limited damage, but the spread fade contradicted live blowout evidence after NYK hit 74 first-half points.

source_notes: Official NBA scorer report uploaded as 20260504_PHINYK_book (2).pdf
